name: Jin Qian
tagline: Researching environmental governance, more-than-human politics, and China's wildlife and food systems.
bio: Jin Qian is a researcher working at the intersection of environmental governance, more-than-human relations, and China's wildlife and food systems. Her recent work examines wild animal farming, ecological civilization, and the politics of nature-based solutions across the Global South. She holds a PhD candidacy at Wageningen University & Research.
longBio:
## Academic and Professional Background

I graduated from Shanghai University of Electric Power with a degree in Computer Science and Technology. After that, I spent several years in the indie music industry and in media, working for both Chinese-language magazines and English-language outlets in Shanghai. I then earned a Master's degree in Environmental Policy from Wageningen University.

## PhD Research

I started my PhD in 2022, having begun my Master's in 2019. My research sits at the intersection of food, the environment, and animals. I apply a social-practices and more-than-human approach to study China's wild animal industry after COVID-19 — specifically the consumption, trade, and production of amphibians and reptiles, which were reclassified from terrestrial to aquatic animals to exempt them from the wildlife ban.

## Projects and Advocacy

Aside from my academic work, I am engaged in knowledge production and animal advocacy in China.

- **Podcast** — I host [有点豆腐 Slightly Tofu](https://slightlytofu.github.io/), a bilingual podcast (with a dedicated English-speaking series) that is currently, as far as I can tell, the only animal rights podcast in China.
- **Lettuce Know** — I am currently building [this directory](https://lettuce-know.pages.dev/) of resources for animal rights activists, indexing academic, ethical, and on-the-ground sources in both Simplified Chinese and English.

---

*How does one go from writing for Shanghai indie music magazines to writing about amphibious beings? She is, honestly, not entirely sure.*

podcast.name: 有点豆腐 Slightly Tofu
podcast.description: Chinese-language podcast on animal rights, veganism, and abolitionism
